title: Art Storage Shelf with Caddies
summary: A two-tier open shelf sized for stacked paper and art caddies, built almost entirely from pocket-holed 1x12 and 1x3 boards.
description:
This is a genuinely small build — one 6-ft board and four 1x3s cover the whole thing — and the only real rule to watch is the cut list's own callback: the bottom shelf's boards must come out exactly 4-1/2" shorter than the top shelf's side boards, or the two tiers won't stack with even overhangs. Everything else is straightforward pocket-hole assembly, trimmed on all four edges for a finished look.

Built as a fixed two-tier shelf rather than with adjustable pins, on the reasoning that art caddies and paper stacks are a known, unchanging size — adjustability solves a problem this piece doesn't have.

cutList.note: Must be 4-1/2" less than the top shelf side boards.
May need to adjust to fit.

steps.title: Build the top shelf bottom
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on all four sides of the smaller 1x12 boards, two per side. Attach to the 1x3 dividers with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Trim the top shelf
steps.body: Measure and cut the side trim pieces to fit, and attach with pocket holes on the underside as well as 1-1/4" brad nails to the 1x3 side trim. Set the top shelf aside.

steps.title: Build the bottom shelf
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on all four sides of the remaining 1x12 boards. Attach the 1x3 side trim pieces with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, hiding them on the underside.

steps.title: Finish the bottom shelf front and back
steps.body: Finish the front and back with 1x3 trim, attaching on the bottom through the predrilled pocket holes and nailing to the side trim with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Join the two shelves
steps.body:
Attach the two shelves inside the legs with 1-1/4" screws or brad nails and glue.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Two-tier art storage shelf for stacked caddies
